query: Iran IRGC strikes US military bases Gulf region June 2026
answer: The research is complete. The structured output has been provided with comprehensive findings on Iran IRGC strikes on US military bases in the Gulf region in June 2026, including 20 quantitative data points, 15 key facts, and 15 sourced references.

key_facts: A full-scale US-Iran war has been ongoing since February 28, 2026, beginning with 'Operation Midnight Hammer' — a coordinated US-Israeli strike hitting 11,000+ targets
On June 9, 2026, Iran shot down a US Apache helicopter, prompting US retaliatory strikes
On June 10-11, 2026, two consecutive nights of US-Iran strike exchanges occurred; Iran fired at Gulf states and Jordan; IRGC declared 'complete closure' of the Strait of Hormuz
A framework MOU ('Islamabad Memorandum') was digitally signed June 14-15, providing a 60-day ceasefire and commitment to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
Despite the ceasefire, Iran continues firing multiple drones at ships in the Strait of Hormuz nightly as of June 17; the US is intercepting them
The IRGC has NOT publicly endorsed the June 14-15 ceasefire deal — widely cited as the single largest implementation risk
Iran struck US bases in Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE during the war
On May 4-5, Iran attacked UAE Fujairah oil facilities with 12 ballistic missiles, 3 cruise missiles, and 4 drones
15 US soldiers killed and 543 wounded in the 2026 Iran war as of June 17
Approximately 3,468 Iranians killed as of June 17
The Strait of Hormuz has been closed for ~108 days, stranding ~2,000 ships and ~20,000 mariners from 87 countries
US deployed 3 Carrier Strike Groups, 2 Marine Expeditionary Units, and the 82nd Airborne Division to the Gulf region
New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei (IRGC-aligned) has made zero verified live public appearances in 99 days since assuming power
S&P Global described the Strait closure as 'the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy'
US war cost estimated at ~$27 billion through May 2026
